response.justification: Bogdan brings strong technical leadership, proven mentoring, and genuine large-scale systems experience, particularly in building high-performance distributed systems. However, there's a critical gap: the role requires 8 years of C++ programming, and his resume shows only ~1.5 years (National Instruments 2014-2015). While his recent infrastructure work (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, microservices) demonstrates cloud competency, his recent trajectory (2021-present) has shifted toward product and tooling rather than deep platform ownership. He's a proven technical leader but not the C++ specialist this role demands.
strengths: Exceptional technical leadership track record: multiple staff roles with demonstrated mentoring, established TDD/refactoring practices, and ability to drive complex projects from chaos to clarity

Direct large-scale systems expertise: built low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second; shipped products to millions (top 10 Apple TV app); event-driven GIS platform on Kubernetes
Cloud and infrastructure fluency: hands-on AWS CDK, microservices architecture, CI/CD pipeline setup, Docker, GitLab administration, proven ability to balance short and long-term infrastructure needs
concerns: Critical C++ gap: resume shows only ~1.5 years of C++ experience against the 8-year minimum requirement; primary languages are JavaScript/TypeScript and DLang, not systems programming

Recent role drift from infrastructure: last 3+ years focused on product (pricing optimization), AI tooling, and full-stack work rather than ownership of a cloud compute platform; infrastructure expertise may be dated

Unclear compute platform specialization: cloud experience is solid but generalist (full-stack DevOps, microservices, Kubernetes) rather than deep ownership of an internal compute platform's technical strategy
